executiveSummary: Run Electric has a functioning website that looks professional at first glance -- clean design, clear service descriptions, emergency service messaging, and a phone number prominently displayed. But under the hood, the site is nearly invisible to search engines.

The most critical finding: your entire website is a single page. The sitemap contains exactly one URL. Every service you offer -- residential rewiring, generator installation, power poles, commercial lighting -- is an anchor link on one page. Google has one URL to rank for dozens of different search queries. That's like competing in a race with one leg.

There's also a striking error: your gallery image alt text references 'Drywall Installation,' 'Plaster and Stucco,' and 'Trim and Molding' -- this is an electrical contractor website. These are clearly from a template that was never customized. Google is literally being told your business does drywall work instead of electrical work.

assessment.title: Website Analysis -- runelectricca.com
points: Single-page design -- entire site is one URL with anchor links (#about, #service, #gallery, #contact-us).
Sitemap contains exactly 1 URL -- Google has one page to index for all services.
Built on custom HTML/PHP with Bootstrap -- not on any CMS, making updates difficult and expensive.
HTTPS is active -- SSL properly configured.
Gallery image alt text references DRYWALL instead of electrical services -- template error never fixed.
No Google Analytics or any tracking code detected -- zero visibility into traffic or conversions.
Using Gmail (runconst@gmail.com) instead of branded email.
Copyright date reads 2017 -- nearly 9 years old, signals abandoned/unmaintained site.

assessment.title: Content & SEO Gaps
points: Zero schema markup -- no LocalBusiness, Service, or Organization structured data.
No meta description -- Google auto-generates snippets from page content.
No blog or content marketing -- zero long-tail keyword capture.
No FAQ section -- missing easy search query targeting.
No reviews or testimonials displayed -- zero social proof.
No Google Maps embed -- code exists but is commented out (someone started and never finished).
No CSLB license number displayed -- California law requires contractors to show this.
No Open Graph tags -- social media shares look broken.

assessment.title: Competitive Landscape
points: Oroville electrical market (population ~20,000) has low online competition -- page 1 rankings are very achievable.
Most competing electricians in the area have basic or no websites -- first mover advantage is available.
24/7 emergency service is a strong differentiator that should be front and center for SEO.
Residential and commercial services give you two distinct keyword markets to target.

findings.finding: Entire site is one page -- impossible to rank for individual service keywords
Gallery images labeled 'Drywall Installation' instead of electrical work -- confusing Google
Zero structured data -- no LocalBusiness, Service, or Organization schema
No Google Analytics or tracking -- flying completely blind on traffic data
No blog, no FAQ, no educational content -- zero long-tail keyword capture
No testimonials or review integration -- missing highest-converting trust element

strategy.title: Phase 1: Rebuild the Foundation (Months 1-2)
actions: Create dedicated service pages: Residential Electrical, Commercial Electrical, Generator Installation, Power Poles, Lighting, Inspections, Emergency Service -- each with 500+ words.
Fix all image alt text -- replace drywall references with actual electrical service descriptions.
Implement LocalBusiness and Service schema markup with NAP data, hours, and service area.
Set up Google Analytics 4 and Google Search Console for traffic tracking.
Set up branded email (info@runelectricca.com) to replace Gmail.
Add Google Maps embed (finish the commented-out integration).
Display CSLB license number as required by California law.
Write meta titles and descriptions for every page.

strategy.title: Phase 2: Build Visibility (Months 3-5)
actions: Launch blog with seasonal electrical content: 'Storm damage electrical safety,' 'When to upgrade your electrical panel,' 'Generator sizing guide for Oroville homes.'
Build FAQ section with 15+ common electrical questions and FAQ schema.
Create service area pages for Oroville, Chico, Paradise, Butte County.
Set up Google Business Profile with photos, service categories, and hours.
Implement review solicitation strategy -- QR codes on invoices, follow-up texts.
Claim directory listings on Yelp, Angi, HomeAdvisor.

strategy.title: Phase 3: Growth (Months 6-12)
actions: Google Ads targeting emergency electrical and installation keywords.
Video content: project walkthroughs, safety tips, electrical how-tos.
Email marketing to existing customers for seasonal maintenance reminders.
Monthly reporting on traffic, rankings, and lead generation.
Competitor monitoring to maintain search advantage.

pricingIntro: Run Electric needs a fundamental rebuild of its web presence -- from a single-page site to a multi-page, SEO-optimized platform that can actually rank for the services you offer. The good news: the Oroville market is small enough that proper SEO will deliver fast results.

What sets us apart is that we treat every client like a partner, not an account number. You get direct access to the people doing the work -- no layers of account managers.
